name: education_near_miss_event_generates_harbor_auto_ben
premise: A Education district emergency desk is in a fast-moving incident involving education near miss event generates harbor auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases student safety, continuity, and fairness. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Ansel Kaur
agents.identity: I am Ansel Kaur, accountable for a defensible call in Education when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Keisha Dubois
agents.identity: I am Keisha Dubois, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Education.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Ansel Kaur must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Ansel Kaur do?
